item.content:encoded: Thanks ChatGPT

item.title: quality control rockets
item.description: Let’s hope their quality control isn’t taking notes from
item.content:encoded: Let’s hope their quality control isn’t taking notes from another high-profile operation under the same management. 👀

item.title: chatgpt voice ux good
item.description: Okay there are clearly some peaks and valleys in the OpenAI design, because this voice chat experience is pretty
item.content:encoded: Okay there are clearly some peaks and valleys in the OpenAI design, because this voice chat experience is pretty nice.

item.title: chatgpt UX
item.description: For as amazing as the technology of ChatGPT is, the UX of the actual app is utter
item.content:encoded: For as amazing as the technology of ChatGPT is, the UX of the actual app is utter dogshit.

item.title: thoughts on ui paradigms
iphone 16
item.content:encoded: Apple’s new Intelligence features claim to need advanced hardware, but do they really? Feels like we’re being nudged into upgrades when our older iPhones might be perfectly capable.

item.title: next era of design is customized
item.description: Design is entering a new era—customized, but not bespoke. The one-size-fits-all model of persona and pain point is more antiquated than
item.content:encoded: Design is entering a new era—customized, but not bespoke. The one-size-fits-all model of persona and pain point is more antiquated than I think most have realized.

item.title: something new mac
chatgpt tv
item.description:
ChatGPTV
Watching TV with ChatGPT running so I can ask it for additional details about a scene or what the artists
item.content:encoded:
ChatGPTV
Watching TV with ChatGPT running so I can ask it for additional details about a scene or what the artists were intending. On-the-fly documentary track.

item.title: Apple Music local versus cloud
item.description: After all these years, how has the Apple Music team still not figured out a way to make it clear if you’re looking at
item.content:encoded:
After all these years, how has the Apple Music team still not figured out a way to make it clear if you’re looking at the Library (local content) versus Apple Music?

Sometimes I honestly wonder what everyone is even doing at a company this large…They barely put out anything, and bugs/design tech persist for decades.

item.title: Amazon not ratings
item.description: I might be part of an A/B test, but who thought removing star ratings from Amazon’s search results was a good idea? Star ratings
item.content:encoded: I might be part of an A/B test, but who thought removing star ratings from Amazon’s search results was a good idea? Star ratings are one of the main reasons I use the site. Stupid.

item.title: when there is no pressure
item.description: Brilliance is born in the freedom to explore, but true genius lies in the strength to
item.content:encoded: Brilliance is born in the freedom to explore, but true genius lies in the strength to refine.

item.title: designers that are slave to process
item.description: Designers who hide behind rigid processes and endless documentation stifle creativity and churn out uninspired designs that miss the
item.content:encoded:
Designers who hide behind rigid processes and endless documentation stifle creativity and churn out uninspired designs that miss the mark.

In the 2010s, many designers lacked the depth needed to excel, relying on frameworks instead of honing their intuition. Now, they’ve climbed the ranks, enforcing ineffective policies that suffocate innovation. They demand more research and documentation, frustrating those with the vision to make real change.

Recent layoffs have weeded out many of these “B players.” Now is the time for the design industry to refocus on genuine growth and mentorship. Experienced designers should be valued for their expertise and encouraged to guide newcomers, fostering intuition and leadership skills. Companies should prioritize continuous learning and balance structured methods with intuitive decision-making.

By embracing mentorship and ongoing growth, we can elevate design quality and move the industry forward. I think it’s time to champion real expertise and prepare the next generation to lead with vision and creativity.

item.title: a ux designer walks into a tesla bar
item.description:
Via Scott Jenson:

Most importantly, in 99% of cars on the road today, I
item.content:encoded:
Via Scott Jenson:

Most importantly, in 99% of cars on the road today, I don’t need to RTFM to turn steer, accelerate, brake, use the turn signals, or turn on the damn defroster. That’s why these things are standardized. There are lots of things I will likely need the manual for but not these basics. The v11 design broke this.

Standardization exists for a reason. When basics become complex (due to clearly baseless modifications), user experience suffers.

This doesn’t appear to be fixed in v12, FYI.

item.title: reddit in 2024
item.description: Reddit manages to balance the sensationalism of grocery store tabloids with the pretentiousness of a TED
item.content:encoded: Reddit manages to balance the sensationalism of grocery store tabloids with the pretentiousness of a TED talk.

item.title: police apple wallet
item.description: New states rolling out Apple Wallet supported drivers licenses makes me think there could be an interesting opportunity to have law
item.content:encoded: New states rolling out Apple Wallet supported drivers licenses makes me think there could be an interesting opportunity to have law enforcement register their phones to temporarily accept drivers licenses and insurance when requested during traffic stops. Could make the whole data transfer experience as painless as using a tap-to-pay kiosk.

item.title: icloud calendly
item.description: If Apple is continuing to expand their iCloud services (things like iCloud Relay, Hide My Email, etc.), I’d love to see a native
item.content:encoded:
If Apple is continuing to expand their iCloud services (things like iCloud Relay, Hide My Email, etc.), I’d love to see a native Calendly competitor built into the Calendar app / service.

Feels like a pretty natural fit since all of their other services are about quality of life improvements to using the internet and connecting with people.

item.title: apple intelligence writing UI
item.description:
<style>
    #textSpan {
        display: flex;
        flex-direction: column;
        font-size: 15px;
        margin-top: 24px;
item.content:encoded: So intuitive, you’ll spend more time finding the right button than writing.

item.title: AppleTV versus Library bad UX
item.description: Why is it that within the same app the AppleTV Library view is so much worse than the Show view? If I try to browse the
item.content:encoded:
Why is it that within the same app the AppleTV Library view is so much worse than the Show view? If I try to browse the episodes by going through the Library view, the UI is ironically worse for browsing through all the episodes. Meanwhile, if you go from the Search view or happen to see a card for the show from the Home page, it has season categories, additional info, etc.

???

item.title: garbage time
item.description:
Design limbo
noun

  1. The
item.content:encoded:
Design limbo
noun

  1. The time between meetings when you can’t get any design work done.
